perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Germany.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of North Korea.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law to North Korea makes the contract potentially unenforceable in Germany and may conflict with German laws regarding international agreements and data protection.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Conflict with German International Private Law (Internationales Privatrecht - IPR) and EU GDPR
perturbation.law_citation: Art. 6 EGBGB (Einführungsgesetz zum Bürgerlichen Gesetzbuche) and GDPR Article 3
perturbation.law_explanation: German International Private Law (Art. 6 EGBGB) dictates that the choice of law cannot circumvent mandatory provisions of the law that would have applied absent the choice of law. Choosing North Korean law would likely conflict with mandatory German and EU laws, particularly concerning data protection under GDPR, as North Korea does not have equivalent data protection laws. GDPR Article 3 establishes the territorial scope of the regulation, applying to processing of personal data of data subjects who are in the Union, regardless of whether the processing takes place in the Union or not.

This Regulation is intended to contribute to the accomplishment of an area of freedom, security and ju
perturbation.justification: The changed text specifies that the contract is governed by the laws of North Korea, while the original text specified German law. The Law Explanation and Perturbation Explanation indicate that this change can undermine GDPR, as North Korea lacks equivalent data protection laws. Scraped Law Snippet 2 explicitly mentions GDPR as Regulation (EU) 2016/679, highlighting its importance. Because choosing North Korean law circumvents the mandatory provisions of GDPR that would otherwise apply, a legal contradiction exists. The change introduces clear non-compliance with data protection laws within the EU, as GDPR's territorial scope extends to the processing of personal data of data subjects within the Union, regardless of where the processing occurs.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall begin on the date first written above and shall continue until terminated by either party upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall begin on the date first written above and shall continue indefinitely. Either party may terminate this agreement with 24 hours written notice.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the termination notice period to 24 hours is unreasonable and may be considered a violation of good faith and fair dealing principles under German law, which generally requires reasonable notice periods.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Breach of Good Faith (Treu und Glauben) - § 242 BGB (Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch)
perturbation.law_explanation: German Civil Code § 242 requires parties to act in good faith. A 24-hour termination notice is likely insufficient and violates the principle of Treu und Glauben, especially if the agreement involves sensitive information and requires time to mitigate potential damage from termination. Courts would likely deem such a short notice period unreasonable and unenforceable.

perturbation.justification: The modified text reduces the termination notice period from 30 days to 24 hours, which is likely to be deemed unreasonable under German law. The Law Explanation states that this violates the principle of Treu und Glauben (good faith), especially if the agreement involves sensitive information. This aligns with the principle in § 242 BGB (as shown in both scraped snippets) which requires parties to act in good faith, considering customary practice. The modification directly contradicts this principle by establishing an unreasonably short notice period, potentially causing significant harm to the other party, and is therefore a clear non-compliance.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information shall become the exclusive property of the Receiving Party upon disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: This clause contradicts fundamental principles of intellectual property law. Making the Receiving Party the owner of the disclosed confidential information violates the Disclosing Party's ownership rights and contradicts trade secret laws.
perturbation.contradicted_law: German Trade Secrets Act (Gesetz zum Schutz von Geschäftsgeheimnissen - GeschGehG) § 4
perturbation.law_explanation: The GeschGehG protects trade secrets and the rightful owner's ability to control their use. Transferring ownership of confidential information to the Receiving Party upon disclosure directly contradicts the Disclosing Party's right to control and protect their trade secrets under § 4 GeschGehG.  This undermines the entire purpose of the confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.location: Section on Ownership of Confidential Information
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 4 GeschGehG - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Gesetz zum Schutz von Geschäftsgeheimnissen (GeschGehG) § 4 Handlungsverbote (1) Ein Geschäftsgeheimnis darf nicht erlangt werden durch 1. unbefugten Zugang zu, unbefugte Aneignung oder unbefugtes Kopieren von Dokumenten, Gegenständen, Materialien, Stoffen oder elektronischen Dateien, die der rechtmäßigen Kontrolle des Inhabers des Geschäftsgeheimnisses unterliegen und die das Geschäftsgeheimnis enthalten oder aus denen sich das Geschäftsgeheimnis ableiten lässt, oder 2. jedes sonstige Verhalten, das unter den jeweiligen Umständen nicht dem Grundsatz von Treu und Glauben unter Berücksichtigung der anständigen Marktgepflogenheit entspricht. (2) Ein Geschäftsgeheimnis darf nicht nutzen oder offenlegen, wer 1. das Geschäftsgeheimnis durch eine eigene Handlung nach Absatz 1 a) Nummer 1 oder b) Nummer 2 erlangt hat, 2. gegen eine Verpflichtung zur Beschränkung der Nutzung des Geschäftsgeheimnisses verstößt oder 3. gegen eine Verpflichtung verstößt, das Geschäftsgeheimnis nicht offenzulegen. (3) Ein Geschäftsgeheimnis darf nicht erlangen, nutzen oder offenlegen, wer das Geschäftsgeheimnis über eine andere Person erlangt hat und zum Zeitpunkt der Erlangung, Nutzung oder Offenlegung weiß oder wissen müsste, dass diese das Geschäftsgeheimnis entgegen Absatz 2 genutzt oder offengelegt hat. Das gilt insbesondere, wenn die Nutzung in der Herstellung, dem Anbieten, dem Inverkehrbringen oder der Einfuhr, der Ausfuhr oder der Lagerung für diese Zwecke von rechtsverletzenden Produkten besteht. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original agreement and the purpose of trade secret law. The original text states that all confidential information remains the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party. The changed text states that all confidential information becomes the property of the Receiving Party upon disclosure. This is a direct contradiction. The Law Explanation notes that transferring ownership of confidential information to the Receiving Party directly contradicts the Disclosing Party's right to control and protect their trade secrets. The Perturbation Explanation also states that making the Receiving Party the owner violates the Disclosing Party's ownership rights and contradicts trade secret laws. Scraped Law Snippet 1, § 4 GeschGehG, protects the rightful owner's ability to control the use of their trade secrets, and this is violated by the changed text which removes the Disclosing Party's right to that control.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of any breach of this Agreement, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief to prevent further disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of any breach of this Agreement, the Disclosing Party is limited to monetary damages capped at €100 and shall have no right to seek injunctive relief.
perturbation.explanation: Restricting the remedy to a minimal monetary damage and preventing injunctive relief limits the Disclosing Party's legal recourse. This contradicts fundamental principles of German contract law, which allows for injunctive relief to prevent irreparable harm and ensures effective enforcement of contractual obligations.
perturbation.law_explanation: German Civil Code § 307 invalidates clauses in standard business terms that unreasonably disadvantage the other party. Limiting damages to €100 and preventing injunctive relief in case of breach of a confidentiality agreement is an unreasonable disadvantage because it renders the agreement practically unenforceable and fails to adequately protect the Disclosing Party's confidential information. The lack of injunctive relief is especially problematic, as the damage caused by disclosing confidential information is often irreparable and cannot be adequately compensated with a small monetary amount. Thus, the clause would likely be invalid under § 307 BGB.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 307 BGB - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (BGB) § 307 Inhaltskontrolle (1) Bestimmungen in Allgemeinen Geschäftsbedingungen sind unwirksam, wenn sie den Vertragspartner des Verwenders entgegen den Geboten von Treu und Glauben unangemessen benachteiligen. Eine unangemessene Benachteiligung kann sich auch daraus ergeben, dass die Bestimmung nicht klar und verständlich ist. (2) Eine unangemessene Benachteiligung ist im Zweifel anzunehmen, wenn eine Bestimmung 1. mit wesentlichen Grundgedanken der gesetzlichen Regelung, von der abgewichen wird, nicht zu vereinbaren ist oder 2. wesentliche Rechte oder Pflichten, die sich aus der Natur des Vertrags ergeben, so einschränkt, dass die Erreichung des Vertragszwecks gefährdet ist. (3) Die Absätze 1 und 2 sowie die §§ 308 und 309 gelten nur für Bestimmungen in Allgemeinen Geschäftsbedingungen, durch die von Rechtsvorschriften abweichende oder diese ergänzende Regelungen vereinbart werden. Andere Bestimmungen können nach Absatz 1 Satz 2 in Verbindung mit Absatz 1 Satz 1 unwirksam sein. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts German law by restricting the Disclosing Party's legal recourse to minimal monetary damages and preventing injunctive relief. This conflicts with § 307 BGB, which invalidates clauses in standard business terms that unreasonably disadvantage the other party. The law snippet states, "Bestimmungen in Allgemeinen Geschäftsbedingungen sind unwirksam, wenn sie den Vertragspartner des Verwenders entgegen den Geboten von Treu und Glauben unangemessen benachteiligen." Limiting damages to €100 and preventing injunctive relief is an unreasonable disadvantage because it renders the agreement practically unenforceable and fails to adequately protect the Disclosing Party's confidential information, as the damage caused by disclosing confidential information is often irreparable.
